channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jazz give Grizzlies a scare, but lose seventh straight
item.description: MEMPHIS (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz are well on their way to their worst record in franchise history, but they're not going down without a fight. Luke Kennard scored 30 points and Ja Morant had 28, helping the Memphis Grizzlies top the Utah Jazz 122-115 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NFL players score a shot at Hollywood through hands-on training sessions with filmmaker Deon Taylor
item.description: SANTA MONICA, Calif. (AP) — Isaac Ukwu's sights were solely set on just playing in the NFL — until two season-ending knee injuries in college served as a harsh reality of football's fragility. The setbacks reshaped Ukwu's perspective, showing him that football was part of him, but not all of him.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oliver Miller, center who helped Arkansas reach Final Four and played 9 NBA seasons, dies at 54
item.description: Oliver Miller, a beefy center who helped Arkansas advance to the Final Four before playing nine seasons in the NBA, has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Source: 49ers hiring QB Mac Jones four years after nearly drafting him
item.description: Quarterback Mac Jones, passed over by the 49ers with the No. 3 pick in the 2021 NFL Draft, agreed Wednesday night to join them as Brock Purdy's backup on a two-year contract, a league source confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: 5th anniversary of COVID cancellations brings back painful memories for teams, NCAA officials
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Robert Morris coach Andrew Toole stood next to the staging area near midcourt Tuesday night, the confetti strewn around him as the Colonials danced, mingled with fans and climbed ladders to cut down the nets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mallory Swanson will miss Chicago Stars' NWSL season opener for personal reasons
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Stars forward Mallory Swanson will miss the team's National Women's Soccer League season opener on Friday night against the Orlando Pride for personal reasons. Coach Lorne Donaldson said Wednesday that he believed Swanson's status was week-to-week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: With Allen's personal and financial life set, Bills QB focuses on unfinished business
item.description: With Josh Allen's personal and financial life set, the NFL MVP is focusing his attention on unfinished business: leading the Buffalo Bills to a Super Bowl title.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rick Pitino wins 1st Big East Coach of the Year award and RJ Luis Jr. is Player of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Add another accolade to Rick Pitino's incredible resume. This was a new one, too — which might come as a surprise. Pitino won his first Big East Coach of the Year award on Wednesday and RJ Luis Jr. took Player of the Year honors after they propelled a resurgent St.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Jose State leads from start to finish in Mountain West tournament opener
item.description: The No. 8 seeded Spartans scored the first 12 points of the game and never trailed in beating Wyoming for the third time this season.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Boys Lacrosse: La Jolla Country Day 17, Rancho Bernardo 4
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Boys Lacrosse in the north county on Tuesday evening with La Jolla Country Day beating Rancho Bernardo 17-4.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers free agency tracker: Deebo Samuel trade official, defensive tackles flee, safety Thornhill to visit
item.description: The NFL's official fiscal year is underway and that makes officials moves such as the 49ers' trade of Deebo Samuel to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Senators, Blue Jackets, Rangers and Bruins lead an East wild-card playoff race full of surprises
item.description: The Ottawa Senators last qualified for the playoffs in 2017, while the Columbus Blue Jackets have only won a round once in more than two decades of existence and none since before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: High school basketball: 10 Chicagoland boys teams playing for IHSA state titles in Champaign this weekend
item.description: The final weekend of the 2024-25 high school boys basketball season in Illinois has arrived, as the Super Sixteen has been whittled down to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Cheating scandal shocks ski jumping, topples Olympic champions and shakes Norway's lofty reputation
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Sign stealing in baseball. Match fixing in soccer. Doping allegations in swimming. Now ski jumping has its own scandal that escalated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bell rolls into Las Vegas on impressive hot streak
item.description: Nothing seemed to be going right at the start of Christopher Bell's relationship with crew chief Adam Stevens. They struggled to get the right setups and results.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stephen A Smith blasts LeBron James for 'weak' approach over Bronny James confrontation
item.description: Stephen A. Smith took aim at LeBron James once again this week over the viral courtside confrontation that took place on Thursday over the ESPN star's comments about James' son, Bronny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Riley Gaines, Laurel Libby rip Gavin Newsom, Hakeem Jeffries over excuses for trans athletes in women's sports
item.description: Maine Rep. Laurel Libby joined Riley Gaines to discuss the excuses by Democrats Gov. Gavin Newsom and Rep. Hakeem Jeffries to allow trans athletes in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Real Madrid beats Atletico on penalties in Champions League. Arsenal, Villa, Dortmund also advance
item.description: Real Madrid beat Atletico Madrid in a Champions League penalty shootout — again — to keep its title defense alive Wednesday and advance to the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utes knocked out of Big 12 Tournament by UCF
item.description: KANSAS CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a tumultuous season for the Runnin' Utes, and it most likely came to an end Tuesday night with an 87-72 loss to UCF in the first round of the Big 12 Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No Donovan Mitchell? No problem for Cavaliers as they tie their team record with 15th straight win
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The wins and accomplishments keep piling up for the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers. They’ve been so frequent that players are having a hard time keeping track. “What did we do? Oh, we clinched the Central Division,” center Jarrett Allen said, chuckling. “That’s something.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, businessman and basketball standout for Louisville and Milwaukee Bucks, dies at 71
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Junior Bridgeman, a basketball standout who led Louisville to a Final Four, starred for the NBA's Milwaukee Bucks and then launched an even more successful career as a businessman with stakes in restaurants, publishing and the Bucks franchise, died Tuesday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PSG ousts Liverpool from Champions League on penalties to join Barcelona, Bayern and Inter in QF
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Gianluigi Donnarumma came through and Paris Saint-Germain defeated Liverpool in a penalty shootout to join Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Inter Milan in the quarterfinals of the Champions League on Tuesday. PSG beat Liverpool 1-0 on the night to level the aggregate score at 1-1.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boys Lacrosse: Carlsbad 11, Granite Hills 7
item.description: EL CAJON (KUSI)- Boys Lacrosse action on Monday night in El Cajon with Granite Hills hosting Carlsbad. The Lancers win a very good game 11-7. Despite Carson Kowalsky, just a junior having a great game, scoring four goals for Granite Hills.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods says he ruptured his Achilles tendon
item.description: It's yet another injury that would seem likely to keep him out of the Masters and perhaps other major championships this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than basketball: UCSD's Division I high tide lifts all athletic boats
item.description: While the great work of the 28-4 UCSD basketball team is the headline, the decade-long journey of the Tritons athletic program to D1 status is the work that athletic director Earl Edwards prides himself on.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz losing skid hits six after 114-108 loss at Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) - Sam Hauser made nine 3-pointers — seven in the third quarter — and finished with a career-high 33 points to lead the short-handed Boston Celtics past the Utah Jazz 114-108 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: George, Filipowski score 25 in Jazz loss to Sixers
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George and Kyle Filipowski each scored 25 points, but the Utah Jazz dropped its fifth straight game, losing at Philadelphia Sunday night, 126-122. Lonnie Walker IV and Quentin Grimes scored 25 points apiece for the victories 76ers.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: DK Metcalf is leaving Seattle for Pittsburgh with a new deal in tow, AP sources say
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks are sending two-time Pro Bowler D.K. Metcalf to Pittsburgh for at least a second-round pick, sources with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Soccer CIF Division IV SoCal State Regional Championship: Pacific Ridge 3, Central Union 2
item.description: The CIF Division IV Boys Soccer SoCal Regional State Championship featured two San Diego Section teams, as the Pacific Ridge Firebirds hosted the Central Union Spartans.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Rugby: Cathedral Catholic 28, La Costa Canyon 17
item.description:
The High School Boys Rugby Championship was held in Del Mar as the Cathedral Catholic Dons hosted the La Costa Canyon Mavericks.
It was the Dons who came out victorious, defeated La Costa Canyon by a score of 28-17, to win their 5th straight Rugby Championship.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-QB in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP DPOY, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Real Salt Lake aims to keep winning as San Diego FC visits for the first time
item.description: Real Salt Lake returns to action at America First Field on Saturday night, facing MLS expansion side San Diego FC for the first time. Coming off a commanding 2-0 win over Seattle last weekend, RSL looks to build momentum and keep their shutout streak alive in front of their raucous home crowd.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks in action against the Predators following overtime victory
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (20-35-8, in the Central Division) vs. Nashville Predators (23-32-7, in the Central Division) Nashville, Tennessee; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah's comeback falls short in 4-3 overtime loss
item.description: CHICAGO (ABC4 Sports) - Connor Bedard scored at 3:10 of overtime to give the Chicago Blackhawks a 4-3 victory against the Utah Hockey Club at United Center on Friday. Bedard circled the net and scored from the slot after going eight games without a goal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco State University cutting men's soccer and 2 other sports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- San Francisco State University announced plans on Thursday to cut three sports programs as part of its budget planning. The announced cuts follow a budget meeting last week that was "particularly difficult," according to an open letter posted by SFSU President Lynn Mahoney.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
